coverage_explanation:
The legal text for CN 2205 defines FlavouredWine (eucn:FlavouredWine) as vermouth and other wines of fresh grapes flavoured with plants or aromatic substances. This class already exists in the TBox with the correct cnHeadingCode hasValue '2205', the correct parent (eucn:Beverage), and the correct disjointness axioms with all sibling beverage classes. No new classes or properties are required.

The legal note additionally provides examples (Marsala all'uovo, Marsala alla mandorla, Crema di Marsala all'uovo — Marsala-wine-based beverages flavoured with egg yolks) as illustrative inclusions. These examples confirm that egg-yolk-flavoured Marsala-based drinks fall under this heading. However, modelling individual product examples as subclasses would be over-specific ontological commitment beyond what the heading-level classification requires; the discriminating criterion (grape-wine base + flavouring with plants or aromatic substances) is already captured by eucn:FlavouredWine and its eucn:cnHeadingCode hasValue '2205'. The flavouring-agent-type detail (e.g. egg yolks vs. botanical plants) is mentioned only as an example and is not a further structural discriminator within heading 2205 itself, so it is not modelled as a restriction here. Coverage is partial because the specific Marsala-egg-yolk product examples are not individually axiomatised, reducing coverage to ~0.55.
